color_profiles.name: Fresh Native Copper
color_profiles.cause: metallic copper surface — characteristic d-band optical absorption

color_profiles.name: Oxidized / Tarnished
color_profiles.cause: formation of cuprite (Cu2O) or tenorite surface layer

color_profiles.name: Patinated / Weathered
color_profiles.cause: formation of malachite, azurite, chrysocolla, or brochantite on surface

visual_notes: Unmistakable copper-red when fresh. Slow oxidation spectrum from red through brown and black to vivid blue-green patina is one of nature's most beautiful chemical sequences. Keweenaw Peninsula specimens show exceptional arborescent habits.
